stimulus: On hot afternoons, asphalt parking lots absorb solar energy and release it as unwanted heat, driving up the temperature of the surrounding ______ a layer of permeable pavers over an existing asphalt lot, either by laying interlocking concrete blocks or pouring a porous resin mix, can substantially reduce this effect.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The period correctly marks the boundary between the first sentence ("On…neighborhood") and the second ("Installing…effect"). The gerund phrase beginning with "Installing" serves as the subject of the second sentence, with "can substantially reduce this effect" as its predicate.

Choice A is incorrect because a semicolon cannot be used in this way; doing so leaves the verb phrase "can substantially reduce" without a subject. Choice C is incorrect because it produces a comma splice. Choice D is incorrect because it yields a confusing sentence implying that installing pavers raises the temperature; it also leaves the verb phrase "can substantially reduce" without a clear subject.
